script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'compete-all-scorers',
  description: 'Beat competitors on ALL axes: DeepTRACE (faithfulness), RACE (quality), DRB-II (published rubric). Run what exists, build what does not, score POLARIS vs ChatGPT/Gemini on each. Honest scoreboard — win or lose stated per axis.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'DeepTRACE', detail: 'wire the key, run the faithfulness scorer that already exists' },
    { title: 'RACE', detail: 'build the 4-dim quality judge, score reports' },
    { title: 'DRB-II', detail: 'stand up the official rubric harness if fetchable' },
    { title: 'Judge', detail: 'Fable: honest multi-axis scoreboard, win/lose per axis', model: 'fable' },
  ],
}

const RULES = `
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports — on EVERY axis.
The operator's directive: use ALL scorers; we must win on all, not cherry-pick the one we pass.

WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS.
Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md — ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped,
Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist. NO FS-Researcher artifact (a background search is hunting the
FS-Researcher paper+benchmark separately) — report FSR as an open gap, never fake a third column.
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md (~35 exist).

THE THREE AXES (orthogonal — a report can win one and lose another):
- DeepTRACE (EXISTS): scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py — 8 citation-faithfulness metrics.
  Judge lock config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml (signed, judge=moonshotai/kimi-k2.6,
  self_rescore fairness policy). Preflight deeptrace_judge_preflight.py HARD-BLOCKS if OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  is unset or provider headroom < 8. The key IS available: source it with
  \`set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a\` (NEVER print it).
- RACE (DOES NOT EXIST — must build): DeepResearch-Bench 4-dim reference-based LLM judge —
  Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by per-task criteria
  vs a reference answer. Build it honestly; do NOT relabel DeepTRACE as RACE.
- DRB-II (EXTERNAL): scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py adapts the official DeepResearch-Bench-II
  rubric harness, but third_party/ is absent. Fetch it if fetchable; if not, say so plainly.

HARD HONESTY RULES:
- If POLARIS LOSES on an axis, say so and by how much. A flattering scoreboard is worse than none.
- Never tune a judge to make us win. Score every system with the SAME judge/tasks (self_rescore).
- Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score the ~35 reports that already exist.
- Cost/rate: paid judge calls on a SHARED OpenRouter account (~217 credits left). If you see HTTP 429,
  SAY SO in blockers; do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.
`

const AXIS_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['axis', 'ran', 'polaris_score', 'competitor_scores', 'polaris_wins'],
  properties: {
    axis: { type: 'string' },
    ran: { type: 'boolean', description: 'did the scorer actually execute end-to-end on real reports' },
    built: { type: 'boolean', description: 'did you have to build the scorer (RACE)' },
    command: { type: 'string' },
    polaris_score: { type: 'string', description: 'REAL measured score, or "unmeasured" with why' },
    competitor_scores: { type: 'string', description: 'ChatGPT scoped/unscoped + Gemini scoped/unscoped, same judge' },
    polaris_wins: { enum: ['yes', 'no', 'partial', 'unmeasured'] },
    margins: { type: 'string', description: 'per dimension, POLARIS vs each competitor, with sign' },
    blockers: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    raw: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

// DeepTRACE first — it exists, just needs the key wired. Fastest real number.
phase('DeepTRACE')
const deeptrace = await agent(
  `${RULES}

AXIS 1 — DEEPTRACE (faithfulness), the one that already exists. Make it RUN.
1. Source the key: \`set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a\` then run
   deeptrace_judge_preflight.py — confirm it now PASSES (it hard-blocked before only because the key
   was unset).
2. Score a substantive real POLARIS report (find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md) AND all 4
   competitor artifacts with the SAME kimi-k2.6 judge (self_rescore). Watch cost — one report is
   ~178 support calls; start with ONE POLARIS + the 4 competitors, not all 35.
3. Report the 8-metric faithfulness scoreboard, POLARIS vs each competitor, and who wins per metric.
   If 429s appear, log them and stop rather than burning credits.`,
  { label: 'compete:deeptrace', phase: 'DeepTRACE', schema: AXIS_SCHEMA },
)

// RACE + DRB-II can proceed in parallel — independent axes, no shared writes to the same scorer.
phase('RACE')
const [race, drbii] = await parallel([
  () => agent(
    `${RULES}

AXIS 2 — RACE (quality). It does NOT exist; BUILD it, honestly.
Implement the DeepResearch-Bench RACE 4-dim reference-based judge (Comprehensiveness, Insight,
Instruction-Following, Readability) with per-task adaptive criteria vs a reference answer, judge via
OpenRouter (reuse the kimi-k2.6 judge lock discipline / self_rescore so it is comparable). Put it under
scripts/dr_benchmark/ in the compete worktree. Then score ONE real POLARIS report + the 4 competitor
artifacts with it. Need a reference/question per task — find the matching one in the repo (the report's
own cp-inputs or the drb task set). Report the 4-dim table, POLARIS vs each competitor, win/lose per
dim. If you cannot obtain reference answers, say so — a RACE score without a reference is not RACE.`,
    { label: 'compete:race-build', phase: 'RACE', schema: AXIS_SCHEMA },
  ),
  () => agent(
    `${RULES}

AXIS 3 — DRB-II (official published rubric). drbii_wrapper.py exists but third_party/ is absent.
Try to stand it up: locate/fetch the official DeepResearch-Bench-II harness + tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl
(check the wrapper for the expected path/URL; the repo may vendor or reference it). If you can fetch it,
run the rubric judge on one POLARIS report + competitors and report the rubric score. If third_party/
is genuinely unfetchable in this environment (no network / not vendored), say so plainly and report
what WOULD be needed — do NOT fabricate a rubric number.`,
    { label: 'compete:drbii', phase: 'DRB-II', schema: AXIS_SCHEMA },
  ),
])

phase('Judge')
const judge = await agent(
  `${RULES}

YOU ARE FABLE, THE INDEPENDENT GATE. Build the honest multi-axis scoreboard.

DEEPTRACE (faithfulness): ${JSON.stringify(deeptrace, null, 2)}
RACE (quality):          ${JSON.stringify(race, null, 2)}
DRB-II (rubric):         ${JSON.stringify(drbii, null, 2)}

Produce the operator's real answer:
1. A single scoreboard: for EACH axis (DeepTRACE, RACE, DRB-II), does POLARIS beat ChatGPT? beat
   Gemini? — yes / no / partial / unmeasured, with the margin. Mark clearly which numbers are REAL
   (actually computed) vs unmeasured/blocked.
2. The blunt bottom line: on how many of the three axes can we HONESTLY claim to beat both competitors
   right now? Where do we LOSE, and by how much?
3. FS-Researcher: state the gap honestly (no artifact yet).
4. The single highest-leverage next action to make "beats all, on all axes" a defensible claim.
Do not flatter. The operator has been burned by a confident wrong thesis before.`,
  { label: 'compete:fable-scoreboard', phase: 'Judge', model: 'fable', effort: 'high' },
)

log(`COMPETE all-axes: DeepTRACE=${deeptrace?.polaris_wins} RACE=${race?.polaris_wins} DRB-II=${drbii?.polaris_wins}`)
return { deeptrace, race, drbii, judge }

deeptrace.polaris_score: unmeasured — no 8-metric number was produced. The scorer CAN score POLARIS (drb_72 report parses to 259 bibliography entries / 415 statements / 245 [N] citations, and corpus_snapshot.json supplies source spans for the kimi support matrix), but a POLARIS-only number is non-comparable under the signed lock's self_rescore policy (which REQUIRES every competitor scored by the same judge) and cannot answer the axis question "does POLARIS beat the competitors." Deliberately did NOT spend shared credits on a number that cannot establish a win. Preflight is GREEN (kimi-k2.6, 21 operational providers >= floor 8, lock signed, exit 0), so a number is *claimable* the moment comparable competitor inputs exist.
deeptrace.competitor_scores: unmeasured for all 4 (ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped, Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped) — cannot be scored comparably with current assets. DeepTRACE needs a Factual-Support Matrix over each cited source's FETCHED content plus a parseable citation->source map. VERIFIED offline ($0) with the repo's own parser: all 4 competitor artifacts yield 0 bibliography entries and 0 real [N] citations (ChatGPT_Scoped 0 bib / 9 false-positive bracket matches; ChatGPT_Unscoped 0/0; Gemini_Scoped 0/0; Gemini_Unscoped 0/0), vs POLARIS 259 bib / 245 cites. ChatGPT cites via opaque unicode `citeturn...view...` UI tokens (no bibliography, unresolvable to fetchable sources); Gemini has a Works-cited list (~32 URLs) but superscript markers that don't map to [N] and no banked source text. No fetched source content exists for ANY competitor, and the third_party/answer-engine-eval scraper DeepTRACE uses to fetch it is absent. Feeding the .md as-is produces all-zero matrices = a fake, POLARIS-flattering scoreboard, which the honesty rules forbid. FSR: no artifact exists (open gap; no third column fabricated).
deeptrace.margins: n/a — no per-dimension margins computable. A same-judge self_rescore comparison could not be produced: POLARIS is scorable but the 4 competitor artifacts cannot be turned into a support matrix (no fetched source content; ChatGPT citations unresolvable in principle). No metric-by-metric POLARIS-vs-competitor deltas exist to sign.
deeptrace.command: set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a; python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check   # -> PASS: kimi-k2.6, 21 providers (floor 8), signed, self_rescore, exit 0. Then offline parser proof (scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py: parse_bibliography/split_statements/citation_columns) over /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md + POLARIS drb_72 report.md -> competitors 0 bib/0 real cites, POLARIS 259 bib/245 cites.
blockers: SCORING BLOCKER (CONFIRMED, offline $0): the 4 competitor artifacts carry no [N]+bibliography structure and no fetched source content, so the DeepTRACE Factual-Support Matrix cannot be built for them with the same kimi judge. Parser proof: ChatGPT_Scoped 0 bib/9 false-positive brackets, ChatGPT_Unscoped 0/0, Gemini_Scoped 0/0, Gemini_Unscoped 0/0; POLARIS drb_72 259 bib/245 cites.

ChatGPT_Scoped/Unscoped cite via opaque unicode `citeturn...view...` UI tokens (private-use separators, no bibliography) that are UNRESOLVABLE to fetchable sources -> scoring these two on faithfulness is impossible in principle without their original browsing session, not merely hard.

third_party/answer-engine-eval (the DeepTRACE source-scraper + gpt-4o statement extractor) is ABSENT from the repo, so even Gemini's ~32 Works-cited URLs cannot be fetched+judged through the intended harness without a live-scraping rebuild.

A same-judge self_rescore comparison (required by the signed lock for any DeepTRACE claim) is therefore unachievable with present assets; a POLARIS-only number would be non-comparable, so none was purchased (preserving the shared ~217-credit budget).
No 429s encountered (only the offline parser + a single preflight GET to OpenRouter ran; no paid judge calls were spent).
FS-Researcher: no artifact present — open gap, third column not fabricated.
deeptrace.raw:
STEP 1 PREFLIGHT (RAN, PASS): `python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check` after `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a` ->
  benchmark judge: moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 | lock signed: True | operational providers: 21 (floor 8) | comparability policy: self_rescore | OK - a DeepTRACE number is claimable under this lock. | EXIT=0.
The earlier hard-block was solely the unset OPENROUTER_API_KEY (now present, len 73). Key never printed.

STEP 2 PARSER PROOF (RAN offline, $0; scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py parse funcs):
  system            bib[N]  stmts  cited_stmts  tot_[N]cites
  ChatGPT_Scoped      0     2000      9(fp)        9(fp)
  ChatGPT_Unscoped    0      162      0            0
  Gemini_Scoped       0      655      0            0
  Gemini_Unscoped     0      320      0            0
  POLARIS_drb72     259      415    200          245
The DeepTRACE scorer (scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, compute_deeptrace_metrics) is a PURE C x S metric function; it needs a citation matrix C and a judge-built support matrix S. Only POLARIS has the inputs (parseable [N]+## Bibliography at report.md:783, plus corpus_snapshot.json 13MB source spans, plus 409 banked kimi verdicts in four_role_settled_verdicts.jsonl). Competitors have neither a parseable citation structure nor fetched source content.

CITATION-FORMAT EVIDENCE:
  ChatGPT: `citeturn24view0turn25view0...` opaque UI tokens (cat -v shows M-nM-^HM-^B private-use separators); 0 bibliography; only ~25 stray URLs in the whole doc; tokens do not resolve to sources.
  Gemini: `#### Works cited` list with ~32 real URLs (e.g. arxiv.org/pdf/2304.11771) but inline superscript citation markers that don't parse as [N], and no banked source text.

HONESTY POSTURE: running the scorer on the competitor .md as-is would emit all-zero citation/support matrices -> unsupported_ratio 1.0, citation accuracy/thoroughness 0.0, i.e. a scoreboard that fakes a POLARIS blowout for a purely structural reason (we lack their sources), not a faithfulness reason. Per the directive ("a flattering scoreboard is worse than none"; "score every system with the SAME judge/tasks"), that number was NOT produced. Files: /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py, /workspace/POLARIS/config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml, /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py, /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md, /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/paid_drb72_deep/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/.

logs: [compete:race-build] failed: You've hit your session limit · resets 6:40pm (UTC)
[compete:drbii] failed: You've hit your session limit · resets 6:40pm (UTC)
[compete:fable-scoreboard] failed: You've hit your session limit · resets 6:40pm (UTC)
